problem_statement: Recurring dimensional non-conformance on fuel injector barrel OD at flange location (datum B). Over a 3-week period (2026-02-24 through 2026-03-11), 7 of 62 parts produced on CNC-02 measured outside the specified 10.00 ±0.05 mm tolerance band, with OD values ranging from 10.07 mm to 10.21 mm. Two batches shipped to preliminary inspection before detection; both were quarantined and returned. Customer was notified per quality agreement clause 4.3.
immediate_action: All suspect parts from CNC-02 runs WO-2026-0101 through WO-2026-0112 quarantined immediately (62 units held). CNC-02 taken offline pending root cause investigation. Incoming and in-process inspection frequency doubled on affected part number. Alternate supplier parts sourced to meet delivery schedule. Customer notified 2026-03-12.

rca.problem_description: Fuel injector barrel OD at flange location repeatedly falls above the upper specification limit of 10.05 mm on CNC-02, despite the same program passing on CNC-01.
rca.root_cause_summary: CNC-02 tool offset compensation was not updated after the end-of-life insert change on 2026-02-20. The maintenance log entry was made but was not linked to the active offset table, so the operator continued using a stale offset that placed the cut 0.12–0.18 mm below the programmed depth, leaving material on the OD.

whys.question: Why were OD dimensions out of tolerance on CNC-02?
whys.answer: The cutting insert was worn beyond its rated life, producing undersized cuts that left excess material on the OD.

whys.question: Why was the worn insert not replaced before it caused out-of-tolerance parts?
whys.answer: The tool life counter was reset after the maintenance event on 2026-02-20, but the tool offset table was not updated to reflect the new insert geometry.

whys.question: Why was the tool offset table not updated when the insert was changed?
whys.answer: The maintenance procedure (MP-CNC-011 Rev C) does not include a mandatory step to verify and update the offset table after an insert change; it only requires logging the change in the maintenance system.

five_whys.identified_root_cause: Maintenance procedure MP-CNC-011 Rev C lacks a mandatory tool offset verification and update step following insert replacement, creating a gap between the physical machine state and the programmed cut parameters.

tasks.description: Quarantine all parts from CNC-02 runs WO-2026-0101 through WO-2026-0112 and conduct 100% CMM inspection to sort conforming from non-conforming units.
Take CNC-02 offline and verify tool offset table against current insert geometry before resuming production. Document offset readings before and after correction.

Revise maintenance procedure MP-CNC-011 to add a mandatory tool offset verification and update step immediately following any insert change. Include a sign-off block requiring operator and QC technician signatures before machine restart.
Conduct refresher training for all CNC operators and maintenance technicians on the revised MP-CNC-011. Record training completion in the training management system.

Implement automated tool offset audit: configure the CNC controller to log offset table values at each job start and compare against the last maintenance-verified baseline. Alert quality if deviation exceeds 0.02 mm. Roll out to CNC-01 through CNC-04.

verification.verification_method: Production monitoring: 30-part sample from each CNC machine (CNC-01 through CNC-04) inspected at 100% on CMM immediately following the next scheduled insert change after implementation of revised MP-CNC-011. Monitoring period: 4 weeks post-training (2026-04-05 through 2026-04-30).
verification.verification_criteria: Zero OD non-conformances on any CNC machine during the 4-week monitoring period following implementation of the revised maintenance procedure and completion of operator training.
verification.verification_notes: 30-part sample from each of CNC-01 through CNC-04 post-insert-change inspection completed 2026-04-14. All 120 parts measured within tolerance (range 9.98–10.04 mm vs spec 10.00 ±0.05 mm). No deviations observed. Tool offset audit log active and functioning. CAPA recommended for closure.
